perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. Recipient agrees, prior to the termination of this Agreement, to provide the Provider with all work products incorporating or derived therefrom or written confirmation of their destruction. [ALTERNATE CLAUSE : Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. Upon the written request of the Provider, Recipient shall promptly return to the Provider any and all Confidential Information without retaining any copies or excerpt thereof except the Recipient may keep one (1) copy for evidentiary purposes only. ]
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees not to use the Proprietary Data in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Proprietary Data for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. Recipient agrees, prior to the termination of this Agreement, to provide the Provider with all work products incorporating or derived therefrom or written confirmation of their destruction. [ALTERNATE CLAUSE : Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. Upon the written request of the Provider, Recipient shall promptly return to the Provider any and all Confidential Information without retaining any copies or excerpt thereof except the Recipient may keep one (1) copy for evidentiary purposes only. ]
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' only in the first sentence of the main clause, it creates an internal contradiction. The first sentence references to the proprietary data, while the rest of the paragraph still refers to the 'Confidential Information', and Proprietary data is not defined, creating contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees not to use the Proprietary Data in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Proprietary Data for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. Recipient agrees, prior to the termination of this Agreement, to provide the Provider with all work products incorporating or derived therefrom or written confirmation of their destruction. [ALTERNATE CLAUSE : Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. Upon the written request of the Provider, Recipient shall promptly return to the Provider any and all Confidential Information without retaining any copies or excerpt thereof except the Recipient may keep one (1) copy for evidentiary purposes only. ]
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Data' in the first sentence of the main clause but does not make that replacement throughout. The 'Contradicted Text' demonstrates this discrepancy within the same location of the text, where the main clause mentions 'Proprietary Data' in the first sentence, but then references 'Confidential Information' later in the same paragraph, creating a contradiction because the terms are now used interchangeably without proper definition. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is effective as of the date first above written and shall remain in full force and effect until [DATE] (*), unless terminated earlier by either Party. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon sixty (60) days written notice to the other Party. The rights and obligations of the Parties under paragraphs 3 and 4 of this Agreement shal l continue beyond termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is effective as of the date first above written and shall remain in full force and effect until [DATE] (*), unless terminated earlier by either Party. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon sixty (60) days written notice to the other Party. The rights and obligations of the Parties under sections 3 and 4 of this Agreement shal l continue beyond termination.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'paragraphs' to 'sections', and there is no sections in the original documents, but Paragraphs. This creates a contradiction as it creates confusion as to what applies post termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is effective as of the date first above written and shall remain in full force and effect until [DATE] (*), unless terminated earlier by either Party. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon sixty (60) days written notice to the other Party. The rights and obligations of the Parties under sections 3 and 4 of this Agreement shal l continue beyond termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text references 'paragraphs 3 and 4', which is clear. The changed text uses 'sections 3 and 4', but the document only contains paragraphs and no sections as described in the Perturbation Explanation. The contradicted text also contains 'sections 3 and 4'. This creates a contradiction because it is unclear whether rights and obligations of paragraphs 3 and 4 or rights and obligations of non-existent sections 3 and 4 continue beyond termination, causing confusion and possible unenforceability, and aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: Any demand, notice or other communication to be given in connection with this agreement shall be given in writing and shall be given by personal delivery, by registered mail or by electronic means of communication addressed to Recipient a s follows: Investigator : [NAME OF DEPARTMENT] [NAME OF BUILDING] [ROOM NUMBER] 1125 Colonel By Drive Ottawa, ON K1S 5B6 Company : [NAME AND COORDINATES]
perturbation.changed_text: Any demand, notice or other communication to be given in connection with this agreement shall be given in writing and shall be given by personal delivery, by registered mail or by digital communications addressed to Recipient a s follows: Investigator : [NAME OF DEPARTMENT] [NAME OF BUILDING] [ROOM NUMBER] 1125 Colonel By Drive Ottawa, ON K1S 5B6 Company : [NAME AND COORDINATES]
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'electronic means of communication' to 'digital communications' introduces inconsistency. Digital communications can encompass a broader range of methods (e.g., social media messages) not necessarily considered formal or reliable under typical contract interpretation. Since the agreement doesn't define 'digital communications', it becomes unclear if informal channels are now acceptable for legal notices, contradicting the initial intention of 'written' communication.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any demand, notice or other communication to be given in connection with this agreement shall be given in writing and shall be given by personal delivery, by registered mail or by digital communications addressed to Recipient a s follows: Investigator : [NAME OF DEPARTMENT] [NAME OF BUILDING] [ROOM NUMBER] 1125 Colonel By Drive Ottawa, ON K1S 5B6 Company : [NAME AND COORDINATES]
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'electronic means of communication' with 'digital communications'. The explanation indicates this introduces inconsistency because 'digital communications' is broader and undefined, potentially including informal channels not suitable for legal notices. Since the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, this broadened and undefined term in the contradicted section creates ambiguity. Thus, it is a strong contradiction as it conflicts with the 'written' requirement, potentially making notices delivered through unwritten means acceptable.
